impact:
LIKELY CONTAMINATED BY ISS-008 — re-measure before investigating.

Original: V2 BASS=0.35/0.50 250-430Hz hump (~3dB) correlates with the MID shift throw, not BASS Q (C27 100n->82n tested and rejected).

2026-07-16: cascade_analysis.py section C shows V2's low-mid (100-400Hz) "excess vs BL=1.00 baseline" is -8.4dB at BL=0.50, driven by the dry-path level error (ISS-008). The BASS-hump captures are BL=0.90/0.95/0.50, i.e. all partial-blend, so their low-mid readings contain the dry-path error. The 250-403Hz region at BL=1.00 reads +2.7 to +4.3dB mean (target/HUGE band) so there IS a real low-mid gap, but its magnitude and BASS-knob correlation cannot be trusted until the dry path is fixed.

Sequence: fix ISS-008 -> re-run comprehensive_report.py -> re-measure this hump -> only then re-open the MID-shift-throw correlation hypothesis. Note the correlation with MID shift may itself be an artifact: MID sits post-blend on V2 (netlists.md V6), so it acts on the contaminated dry+wet sum.
title: V2 BASS 250-430Hz hump at low BASS settings
